name: Yum Hoi Mang-pu - Thai Mussel Salad.
recipeInstructions:
1. Select only good quality, plump, fresh mussels, shelled and debearded.
2. Put the raw mussels into a strainer and drain well.
3. Wash the lemongrass stalk, trim off the leaves and woody top portion and discard. Slice the stalks into fine rings using the tender lower portions only.
4. Peel the shallots and slice fine.
5. Wash the mint leaves and use the leaves whole.
6. Wash the hot fresh Thai chili peppers (or substitute milder chili if desired) and lightly crush with a heavy object or the flat of a knife.
7. Bring a pot of water to a boil and blanch the mussels using the strainer, quickly in the boiling water.
8. Put the mussels into a bowl, let cool, then add lime/lemon juice and fish sauce and mix well.
9. Add the sliced lemongrass, sliced shallots, crushed chili peppers, and mint leaves; lightly toss together.
10. Serve on a platter lined with lettuce leaves and garnish with extra mint leaves and slices of the mild red chili pepper. Serve any remaining lettuce leaves on the side as an accompaniment.
